text:
There once was an old owl named Olive who lived deep in an oak tree. Every dawn, Olive loved to watch the world wake up. One autumn morning, a peculiar little squirrel named Rupert fluttered by on a leaf.

Rupert couldn't talk, but his eyes twinkled with mischief. "Good morning, Olive!" he chirped.

"Oh hello," replied Olive, her voice a serene whisper. "Do you have any ideas for today?"

"Lemme think..." Rupert scampered around, collecting acorns. "Perhaps I could help you with your acorn hoard?"

"A wise choice," Olive said warmly.

As the day went on, Rupert carried treasures home to organize them. His skills were impressive. Olive laughed every time Rupert stuffed acorns into neat cups.

Late afternoon, though, Olive felt tired. "Maybe I'll rest a bit."

"Then I'll bake us some eggsparsness!" suggested Rupert.

Olive nestled underneath an old map. There was a small glaze
